dialogue.utterance: Unit 4, respond to a chest pain at 18th Street.
Hi, I'm Ashley, a paramedic. What brought you to call 911?
Uh, I've had chest pain and trouble breathing for almost two days. It feels tight.
Can you state your name for us?
My name is Sarah.
What is your date of birth?
It's March 12, 1991, although I'm not sure.
We'd like to assess your airway. Can you keep it open for us?
Airway is patent. No signs of obstruction.
I'm going to listen to your breathing now.
Breathing feels heavy. I have to work for each breath.
Respirations are 28 per minute and deep. Bilateral breath sounds are clear.
We'll check your circulation. Any leg problems affecting your movement?
I sometimes feel numbness. I can feel them but not move them well.
Peripheral pulses are strong. Skin is warm and dry, and no edema is seen. Blood pressure is currently trending lower.
Any other symptoms besides the tightness and inability to move legs?
Tightness does spread to my back. No pain or traumas. No abdominal pain.
How severe would you rate the tightness on a scale of one to ten?
It's an 8.
Does the tightness get worse with certain actions?
It gets worse when I breathe fast.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Can I examine your pupils?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Go ahead.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are equal and reactive.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Gross visual examination
dialogue.utterance: Your face appears symmetrical. Any facial numbness or weakness?

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Gross visual examination
dialogue.utterance: No.
Any rashes or changes in complexion?
No. Skin looks normal.
No rashes or bruises visually noted.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Gross motor and sensory function
dialogue.utterance: Can you tell if you can move your hands and feet?

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - Gross motor and sensory function
dialogue.utterance: I can feel my hands and feet, but my feet won't move as well. It's like tingling.
No signs of swelling in the extremities.
Any stomach discomfort?
Just chest and legs.

dialogue.micro_intent: heart and lungs exam - Heart Sounds
dialogue.utterance: Has the tightness or pressure shown any change over time?

dialogue.micro_intent: heart and lungs exam - Heart Sounds
dialogue.utterance: Just pressure and tightness. No pain change.
Tachypnea consistent with anxiety. No signs or symptoms of trauma noted.
Your pulse is 124.
Breathing 28 times a minute, deep, but no signs of labored breathing.
Blood pressure is 121 over 88.
SpO2 shows 99%.
Blood sugar is at zero currently. That's very low.
I'll run an EKG now.
EKG shows sinus tachycardia with occasional PVCs. No other acute changes noted.
I will start an IV for fluid.
IV in the left hand with normal saline is established.
Blood sugar is now at 155 mg/dL. It seems better now.
Pulse is at 112 now.
Blood pressure is 116 over 72.
Per protocol 2-1 for suspected cardiac chest pain. Let's get you ready to transport.
IV functioning and fluids are running normally.
Pulse at 108.
Blood pressure up to 128 over 89.
SpO2 stays at 99 and RR at 28.
Transport destination is Henrico Doctors' Hospital - Forest.
I understand.
Partner, make sure she's secure for transport.
Secure and ready to move.
ED staff, 28-year-old female with chest pain and anxiety. EKG shows no acute changes.
History of panic attacks and hypokalemia noted. No jugular venous distension.
Vital signs improved after IV administration. Full sensation but motor weakness in extremities.
No trauma to the spine or neck observed. Patient is alert and oriented to person, place, time, and event.
All protocols 2-1 followed. All care given and documented.
Any yellow or pale skin?
No.
No jaundice noted. Skin color normal.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - last oral intake
dialogue.utterance: What did you eat or drink in the past day?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - last oral intake
dialogue.utterance: Small meal this morning.
Does the tightness spread to your arms or shoulders?
It's just in the center.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - pertinent medical history
dialogue.utterance: Any other episodes like this before?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - pertinent medical history
dialogue.utterance: Yes, two months ago, and it was attributed to potassium.
Skin turgor is normal.
Any other stomach issues?
No significant pain or discomfort.
Bowel sounds are absent. No signs of distension.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - allergies
dialogue.utterance: Any allergies?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - allergies
dialogue.utterance: Allergies to penicillin and amoxicillin.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - medications
dialogue.utterance: What medications are you currently using?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - medications
dialogue.utterance: I use methadone.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo secondary assessment - head and neck exam
dialogue.utterance: No mass or swelling in the head or neck area.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo secondary assessment - ears nose and throat
dialogue.utterance: No discharge or swelling in the ears or throat.
Any abnormalities in your mouth?
Nothing unusual.
No bleeding or foreign bodies noted in the mouth.
Any swallowing or voice troubles?
No.
No signs of airway obstruction or stridor.
Can you try moving your toes now?
They flicker, but not fully. It's like strange nerve feelings.
Sensation confirmed in both arms and legs. Likely from hypokalemia effects.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - present signs symptoms
dialogue.utterance: Any other symptoms besides the chest tightness?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - present signs symptoms
dialogue.utterance: No. Just tightness. No other pain.
No thoracic asymmetry or observed abnormal movement.
Any pain going into the arms or shoulders?
No, it remains in the center.
Breath sounds clear and equal between both sides.
Any bowel movement or urination changes?
Not really.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - events leading
dialogue.utterance: Did you do anything like lifting or exert before this began?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo history of present illness - events leading
dialogue.utterance: No, it was a gradual onset.
Destination hospital remains Henrico Doctors' Hospital - Forest.
Stretcher secured. Patient remains stable.
GCS was noted at 15 (eye=4, verbal=5, motor=6).
No bruise pattern or other visible injuries reported in ePCR.
Trauma history does not indicate injury.
No reported rashes, discoloration, or edema sighted.
All assessments, procedures, and documentation finalized.
Patient ready for transport. Condition stable.
